pairs.question: Who is Gregory Lynn Geoffroy and what positions did he hold in higher education?
pairs.answer: Gregory Lynn Geoffroy is an American higher education administrator and chemistry professor who served as the 14th president of Iowa State University from 2001 to 2012. He previously held leadership roles at Pennsylvania State University, including assistant professor of chemistry (1974-1978), associate professor (1978-1982), full professor (1982-1988), head of the Chemistry Department (1988-1989), and dean of the Eberly College of Science (1989-1997). He also served as Senior Vice President for Academic Affairs and Provost at the University of Maryland, College Park (1997-2001) before becoming president of Iowa State. After leaving Iowa State, he became chairman of the board of trustees at Ashford University.

pairs.question: What are the key educational milestones in Gregory Lynn Geoffroy's academic career?
pairs.answer: Gregory Lynn Geoffroy earned his Bachelor of Arts (BA) from the University of Louisville in 1968. He then served in the United States Navy from 1969 to 1970. After his military service, he completed his PhD in chemistry at the California Institute of Technology in 1974.

pairs.question: How did Gregory Lynn Geoffroy progress through his academic ranks at Pennsylvania State University?
pairs.answer: Geoffroy began as an assistant professor of chemistry at Pennsylvania State University in 1974, holding this position until 1978. He was then promoted to associate professor from 1978 to 1982. In 1982, he became a full professor and remained in that role until 1988, when he was appointed head of the Pennsylvania State Chemistry Department. He served in this role for one year before becoming dean of the Eberly College of Science in 1989.

pairs.question: What administrative leadership roles did Gregory Lynn Geoffroy hold after leaving Pennsylvania State University?
pairs.answer: After leaving Pennsylvania State University in 1997, Geoffroy served as Senior Vice President for Academic Affairs and Provost at the University of Maryland, College Park until 2001. He then became the 14th president of Iowa State University, a position he held from 2001 to 2012. After his presidency, he assumed the role of chairman of the board of trustees at Ashford University.

pairs.question: Create a timeline of Gregory Lynn Geoffroy's career from 1968 to 2012.
pairs.answer: 1968: Earns BA from the University of Louisville. 1969-1970: Serves in the United States Navy. 1974: Earns PhD in chemistry from California Institute of Technology and becomes an assistant professor at Pennsylvania State University. 1974-1978: Assistant professor at Penn State. 1978-1982: Associate professor at Penn State. 1982-1988: Full professor at Penn State. 1988-1989: Head of the Pennsylvania State Chemistry Department. 1989-1997: Dean of the Eberly College of Science at Penn State. 1997-2001: Senior Vice President for Academic Affairs and Provost at the University of Maryland, College Park. 2001-2012: President of Iowa State University.

pairs.question: What was Gregory Lynn Geoffroy's role at the University of Maryland, College Park?
pairs.answer: From 1997 to 2001, Gregory Lynn Geoffroy served as Senior Vice President for Academic Affairs and Provost at the University of Maryland, College Park. In this role, he was responsible for overseeing academic affairs and serving as the chief academic officer of the university.
